sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a frequent water skier, presented with right knee pain that began after a skiing accident on the weekend, where he twisted his knee while landing a jump.
The pain has been present for five days, and he has not experienced any numbness or tingling in his foot.
He has been taking over-the-counter Aleve twice a day and applying a cold pack, which has provided some relief.
The patient denies any history of previous knee injuries and has a history of tonsillectomy.
Past medical history: The patient has a history of hypertension, for which he is taking lisinopril 20 mg daily.
His blood pressure has been well-managed, with readings of 125/80 at home and 120/77 in the office today.
The patient denies any smoking, alcohol, or drug use.
Review of systems: [NOT MENTIONED]
Current medications: Lisinopril 20 mg daily.
Objective:
Vital signs: The patient's blood pressure is 120/77.
Physical exam: The physical exam revealed ecchymosis, edema, and an effusion present on the right knee.
There is pain to palpation of the right medial knee.
The patient has decreased range of motion, negative varus and valgus test, negative Lachman sign, palpable dorsalis pedis and posterior tibial pulse, and no lower extremity edema.
Test Results: [NOT MENTIONED]
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has a medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee, confirmed by clinical exam findings.
Problem list: Medial collateral ligament strain of the right knee; Hypertension.
Plan:
I prescribed meloxicam 15 mg once a day to help reduce swelling and pain in the right knee.
I also ordered an x-ray of the right knee to rule out any fractures.
I referred the patient to physical therapy to strengthen the muscles around the knee and prevent future injuries.
I advised the patient to rest the knee for a while to avoid further injury.
Follow-up: The patient will follow up with me after the results of the x-ray and to assess the progress of the physical therapy.
I also ordered a lipid panel to check the patient's lipid levels.
A tetanus shot was administered as a health maintenance measure.
